brands.name: Maaco
brands.notes: Auto painting and collision repair franchise founded in 1972, owned by Driven Brands; actively recruiting multi-unit franchisees across the US (minimum 3-unit commitment), but the franchised system has contracted in recent years. Ad fund is a flat fee (greater of $1,200/week or the local market budget) rather than a percentage of sales.

riskFlags.title: Sustained unit contraction
riskFlags.detail: Per the 2025 FDD, US franchised units fell from 411 at the start of 2022 to 363 at the end of 2024 (net -13, -22 and -13 in successive years), with no company-owned units.

riskFlags.title: High historical franchisee turnover and complaints
riskFlags.detail: Unhappy Franchisee documents franchisee complaints alleging heavy owner turnover; FDD-derived analysis cited there shows 136 of 587 franchise agreements (23%) ended in termination, non-renewal, transfer or ceased operations between 2013 and 2015, plus lawsuits between Maaco and former franchisees including franchisee counterclaims alleging misrepresentation.

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes Maaco discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD, which prospective buyers should review.

riskFlags.title: Unanswered BBB complaints
riskFlags.detail: The Better Business Bureau profile for Maaco Franchising, Inc. (Charlotte, NC) notes the business failed to respond to 7 complaints filed against it.
riskFlags.source: Better Business Bureau — Maaco Franchising, Incorporated

nationwide.source: Maaco franchise development site — Available Territories
nationwide.note: Franchisor states it is 'focusing on multi-unit growth opportunities across the country with a minimum of three units commitment to start' (398 locations US/Canada cited); no state exclusions published.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Listed as 'cash requirement'; net worth requirement is $1,000,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Low end reflects conversion/retrofit of an existing auto facility.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end reflects ground-up build; 2025 FDD review shows $728,500–$3,994,000 for ground-up/non-auto retrofit formats.

franchiseFee.note: Standard initial franchise fee; consistent with Vetted Biz FDD summary.

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
royaltyPercent.note: 8% of gross receipts; reduced to 4% for the first 6 months of a newly developed center.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average 2024 gross receipts of 317 established franchised centers open 2+ years; top half averaged $2,226,532 vs $1,001,411 for the bottom half. Revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
totalUnits.note: US franchised units at 2024 year-end per 2025 FDD (0 company-owned); Entrepreneur lists 379 and the franchisor cites 398 incl. Canada.

closureCount.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
closureCount.note: Net decline in franchised units during 2024 per 2025 FDD; system declined 411→363 over 2022–2024.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 7–8 employees required to run a location.

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter — Maaco Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/26/maaco-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

multiUnitAvailable.source: Maaco franchise development site — Available Territories (https://www.maacofranchise.com/available-territories/)
multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchisor focuses on multi-unit deals with a minimum 3-unit commitment.

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur shows -10% unit growth; FDD shows net unit declines 2022–2024.

brands.name: Big O Tires
brands.notes: Tire and auto-service franchise (founded 1962, franchising since 1982), part of TBC Corp., with 460+ franchised stores in 24 states and active development in priority and available growth markets. Royalties are charged on a sliding matrix by product category rather than a single flat rate.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee group lawsuit over royalty and program changes
riskFlags.detail: About 20 current and former California franchisees sued Big O Tires and its parent companies in December 2009 alleging breach of franchise agreements, fraud in the inducement, restraint of trade and unfair competition after post-2005 ownership changes that franchisees said raised effective royalties from 2% to 6% and forced new system purchases.
riskFlags.source: Tire Review — Big O Tire, Parent Firms Sued by Franchisees

riskFlags.title: Historically high SBA loan default rate
riskFlags.detail: A 2012 analysis reported a 23% SBA loan default rate among Big O Tires franchisees and a decline from ~523 stores (2008) to 'more than 480'; dated figures from the 2000s lending cohort, but relevant to downside risk.
riskFlags.source: Unhappy Franchisee — BIG O TIRES Franchise Complaints

riskFlags.title: Franchisee fraud-in-inducement suit decided for franchisor
riskFlags.detail: A franchisee's 2009 California suit alleging exaggerated earnings claims and concealment of failures was rejected because the disclosure document contradicted each claim; illustrates litigation history and the importance of relying on the FDD rather than sales statements.
riskFlags.source: ILN IP Insider — Franchisee Who Ignored His Disclosure Document Loses Lawsuit

liquidCapitalMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — Big O Tires (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/big-o-tires/282135)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur 2026 listing; FranchisePayback's 2025 FDD summary shows $376,000 low end — range depends on build type.

totalInvestmentMax.note: Entrepreneur 2026 listing; FranchisePayback's 2025 FDD summary shows $1,573,000 high end.

franchiseFee.source: FranchisePayback — Big O Tires FDD, Costs & Fees (https://www.franchisepayback.com/franchise/big-o-tires)
franchiseFee.note: Standard initial franchise fee for a new store; Entrepreneur shows $10,000–$17,500 depending on circumstances, and the fee is waived for qualified veterans/first responders on a first unit.

royaltyPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — Big O Tires (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/big-o-tires/282135)
royaltyPercent.note: Top standard rate; royalties are assessed on a sliding matrix by product/sales category, roughly 2%–5% of gross sales (Entrepreneur lists 3.5%–5%).

adFundPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — Big O Tires (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/big-o-tires/282135)
adFundPercent.note: Entrepreneur lists 4.5% ad royalty; other FDD summaries cite ~4.9% combined marketing obligations.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average unit gross revenue per Item 19 of the 2025 FDD as summarized by VetMyFranchise; top-third US stores averaged $2,961,487. Revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — Big O Tires (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/big-o-tires/282135)
totalUnits.note: Franchisor's development site cites 'more than 460 franchised locations in 24 states'.

veteranDiscount.note: First-unit franchise fee waived for qualified US military veterans and first responders.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Entrepreneur lists exclusive territories as available; franchise agreement licenses a specific approved location.

brands.name: The Learning Experience
brands.notes: Large early-education/childcare franchise (founded 2001, franchising since 2003) recruiting nationally via tle-franchising.com, with record 2024 openings and 386 franchised plus 31 company-owned centers at 2024 year-end. High-investment, real-estate-heavy model; the widest investment figures include ground-up development formats.

riskFlags.title: Allegations of misleading financial presentations in franchise sales
riskFlags.detail: An employee review and complaint-site materials allege TLE's sales process used non-standard metrics (EBITAR rather than EBITDA) and 'Project Cost' documents that understated startup and operating expenses; these are unadjudicated allegations, but a franchisee due-diligence report on the brand's complaints and litigation history is separately marketed by FranchiseComplaints.org.
riskFlags.source: Glassdoor employee review of The Learning Experience

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes The Learning Experience discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD; prospective franchisees should review Item 3.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz — The Learning Experience Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Very high and wide total investment range
riskFlags.detail: Initial investment runs from roughly $780,799 to $5,608,799 depending on real-estate approach — far above typical childcare franchise norms — so financing exposure is substantial.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — The Learning Experience

nationwide.source: The Learning Experience franchise development site
nationwide.note: Franchisor actively recruits US-wide ('hundreds of TLE centers under development still in need of the right franchisee', researching 'high-growth areas around the country'); no published state exclusions or open-state list.

liquidCapitalMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — The Learning Experience (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/learning-experience-academy-of-early-education-the/309890)
liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement; net worth requirement is $350,000.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — The Learning Experience (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/learning-experience-academy-of-early-education-the/309890)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Matches 2025 FDD low end for the standard leased-center format.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — The Learning Experience (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/learning-experience-academy-of-early-education-the/309890)
totalInvestmentMax.note: High end includes ground-up/real-estate development formats; 2025 FDD standard leased format tops out at $1,477,299.

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — The Learning Experience (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/learning-experience-academy-of-early-education-the/309890)

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — The Learning Experience Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/04/the-learning-experience-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

adFundPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — The Learning Experience Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/04/the-learning-experience-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
adFundPercent.note: Brand Awareness Fund of 1% of gross revenue, which can rise to up to 2.5%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter — The Learning Experience Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/04/the-learning-experience-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average 2024 gross sales of 232 mature franchised centers (open 48+ months); median $2,160,607. Revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.source: Franchise Chatter — The Learning Experience Franchise Review 2025 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/12/04/the-learning-experience-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
totalUnits.note: 2024 year-end per 2025 FDD: 386 franchised + 31 company-owned; Entrepreneur cites ~450 current locations.

isGrowing.note: +60 net franchised centers in 2024 (record openings); Entrepreneur shows +42% three-year unit growth.

brands.name: KidStrong
brands.notes: Fast-growing kids' physical/character development training franchise (founded 2015 in Texas, franchising since 2019) with 150+ centers and active recruiting; franchisor's site highlights CA, CO, UT and WI as featured open markets. Average unit revenue is omitted because third-party FDD summaries state the FDD does not disclose a system AUV, conflicting with the franchisor's marketing claim.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting AUV claims between franchisor marketing and FDD summaries
riskFlags.detail: KidStrong's franchise site advertises an Average Unit Volume of $1,407,485 (footnoted), while FDD review services state the FDD 'does not disclose the average unit volume' and cite materially lower revenue figures (~$716K); prospects should verify Item 19 directly before relying on marketing figures.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback — KidStrong FDD, Costs & Fees (vs. franchise.kidstrong.com)

riskFlags.title: BBB consumer complaints over memberships and refunds
riskFlags.detail: BBB complaints against KidStrong Inc. describe membership cancellation friction and refusal of refunds (e.g., a denied summer-camp refund after a child's medical emergency); consumer-level rather than franchisee-level, but relevant to brand reputation.
riskFlags.source: Better Business Bureau — KidStrong Inc. complaints

riskFlags.title: Young, rapidly scaling system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2019 with ~273% three-year unit growth per Entrepreneur; rapid scaling of a young system carries execution and support risk.

statesOpen.note: Featured as a target/available market on the official KidStrong franchise development site; other markets may also be available on inquiry.
Featured as a target/available market on the official KidStrong franchise development site; other markets may also be available on inquiry.
Featured as a target/available market on the official KidStrong franchise development site; other markets may also be available on inquiry.
Featured as a target/available market on the official KidStrong franchise development site; other markets may also be available on inquiry.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement; net worth requirement is $500,000.

royaltyPercent.note: Standard royalty 7%, rising to 8.5% in some tiers per Entrepreneur (7%–8.5%).

adFundPercent.note: Ad royalty per Entrepreneur; a separate 1% technology fee also applies.

totalUnits.note: Per Entrepreneur 2026; FranchisePayback's 2025 FDD summary shows 121 franchised + 10 company-owned.

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD summarized by third-party FDD services.

veteranDiscount.note: 15% off the franchise fee for veterans.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Franchisees receive an exclusive territory where no other KidStrong centers can operate.

brands.name: British Swim School
brands.notes: Low-investment swim-lesson franchise (founded 1981, franchising since 2011, owned by Buzz Franchise Brands) that rents existing pools instead of building facilities; actively selling territories with 19 US states explicitly listed and 'territories for sale in most states.' Royalty (10%) is high relative to franchise norms, offset by the low entry cost.

riskFlags.title: Above-norm 10% royalty
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees pay 10% of gross sales in royalties plus a 2% marketing fund — well above the 4–7% royalty typical across franchising — which materially affects unit economics despite the low initial investment.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue dispersion; new territories ramp slowly
riskFlags.detail: Item 19 cohort data show 2023-opened outlets averaged $187,319 and 2022-opened outlets $244,042 versus $608,655 for pre-2019 outlets, with very wide ranges within cohorts — newer owners should expect materially lower early revenue.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes British Swim School discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD; FranchiseComplaints.org maintains court-record documentation on British Swim Centers Franchising LLC.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz — British Swim School Franchise Insights

liquidCapitalMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement; net worth requirement is $150,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Low investment because lessons are run in rented existing pools (no build-out).

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
franchiseFee.note: Single-territory fee; veteran discount applies to the single territory fee.

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025 (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/05/06/british-swim-school-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

adFundPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025 (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/05/06/british-swim-school-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025 (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/05/06/british-swim-school-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 (per 2025 review of the FDD): average 2023 gross revenue of 102 US outlets open and operating 9+ months; cohort averages range from $187,319 (2023 openings) to $608,655 (pre-2019 outlets). Revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
totalUnits.note: Locations per Entrepreneur 2026; the FDD counted 189 franchised outlets at 2023 year-end (0 company-owned), so counts differ by location-vs-territory methodology.

yearFounded.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
yearFounded.note: Started in Manchester, England; US-based franchising under Buzz Franchise Brands.

yearFranchising.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025 (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/05/06/british-swim-school-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

exclusiveTerritories.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
exclusiveTerritories.note: Territories defined demographically (20,000+ children in medium-to-high income brackets) per franchisor site.

absenteeOwnership.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)

canRunFromHome.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)
canRunFromHome.note: Home-based administration; lessons delivered at rented pool facilities.

canRunPartTime.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory — British Swim School (https://www.entrepreneur.com/franchises/directory/british-swim-school/334339)

isGrowing.source: Franchise Chatter — British Swim School Franchise Review 2025 (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2025/05/06/british-swim-school-franchise-review-2025-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
isGrowing.note: Franchised outlets grew 112→136→189 over 2021–2023 per FDD; Entrepreneur shows +104.5% three-year growth.

brands.name: i9 Sports
brands.notes: Home-based youth recreational sports league franchise (founded 2002, franchising since 2003, HQ Tampa/Riverview FL) with one of the lowest entry costs in the category; territories are ZIP-code-configured and protected, available throughout the US except ND, MT and WY per its Franchise Direct listing. Royalty is 7.5% of network revenues plus a 2% brand fund and a 3% local advertising minimum.

riskFlags.title: Weak consumer review ratings and refund complaints
riskFlags.detail: i9 Sports averages 2.2/5 across 553 Yelp reviews, with recurring complaints about denied refunds, cancelled classes and poor communication at some franchise locations; BBB complaints echo similar themes. Customer-level rather than franchisee-level risk.

riskFlags.title: Litigation disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes i9 Sports discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD; past federal cases include Soundkids Productions, Inc. v. i9 Sports Corporation (contract dispute).
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz — i9 Sports Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Wide performance spread tied to venue count
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 registration revenue ranged from $39,030 to $1,745,661 across 224 full-year units (median $360,398); earlier FDD data show single-venue operators averaging ~$132K vs ~$946K for 6+ venue operators — scale matters greatly.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026

liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement; net worth requirement is $100,000.

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
royaltyPercent.note: 7.5% of Network Revenues, minimum $425–$450/month; plus a required local advertising spend of at least 3% of Network Revenues.

adFundPercent.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
adFundPercent.note: Brand Fund of 2% of Network Revenues (minimum $275/month) per the 2025 FDD; Entrepreneur lists 2%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: average 2024 registration revenue of 224 franchise units operated the full calendar year; median $360,398, range $39,030–$1,745,661. Registration revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.note: Per Entrepreneur 2026; FDD shows 264 franchised units at 2024 year-end (0 company-owned).

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 1 employee minimum; seasonal coaches/officials engaged per program.

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)

veteranDiscount.note: 10% off the franchise fee for veterans.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Protected territories configured by ZIP code based on under-14 population density.

canRunFromHome.note: Home-based business; programs run at rented community venues.

isGrowing.source: Franchise Chatter — i9 Sports Franchise Review 2026 (2025 FDD) (https://www.franchisechatter.com/2026/03/18/i9-sports-franchise-review-2026-costs-fees-news-average-revenues-and-or-profits/)
isGrowing.note: Net franchised unit additions of +26, +27 and +19 in 2022–2024 per FDD; Entrepreneur shows +41.6% growth.
